item.title: The story of a badass baker
item.description:
https://twitter.com/LoreKeating/status/1615374462978760704
https://www.titanicinquiry.org/BOTInq/BOTInq06Joughin01.php EDIT: Lots of people asking about suction from the ship going down (shut it, you in the back): none of the survivors reported any suction. The ship had broken up which reduced the mass being dragged under, blah blah water displacement I don't know the science of it all, but there was no suction. As for the liquor keeping him alive, I've heard that others corroborated having seen him drinking, and he did in fact survive, so whether it was thanks to the alcohol or in spite of it, is moot. PS: Thanks for the accolades! (originally posted by @KalliJ on 2023-01-19 12:12:37)

item.title: Suck it, Tax jerks!
item.description:
It took the CRA (we're in Canada) the better part of a year to do the audits and they withheld money from our tax returns in the meantime. Not only did we get more than that back on the last one, we got a bit more back on this one PLUS INTEREST. Yeah, I'm petty. But I also feel fucking vindicated. I've lost sleep over this. It was never a question of wrongdoing. It was because we netfile that they wanted the proof of what we were claiming. It just took them nine months to look at it and we couldn't really afford to wait for the money they were sitting on because my husband works in the film industry and was affected by the strike, and I only work part-time because I'm about to have my second discectomy at the beginning of February. So I just wanted to share a big ol' WOOHOO now that the audits are over... just in time for Tax Season 2024 lol
Here's the reason we get up and go to work. Holy shitballs, I'm so happy I could plotz.
item.dc:creator: ImmortalChild

item.title: Wild dogs of South America
item.description:
This is a look at the ten delightful canine species native to Central and South America.
Due to similar apperances are most named as if they were true foxes or wolves, but taxonomically they are neither. Instead the ten species form their own branch, the Cerdocyonina.
The largest is the solitary Maned Wolf. From central Brazil to northern Argentina it hunts smaller mammals, birds and fish, yet half of the diet consists of vegetables and fruits. With a shoulder height of 110 cm (43,3 in) is the Maned Wolf not only largest among the Cerdocyonina but also the tallest wild canid in the world.
The Short-eared dog is an extremly rare sight, for the small population lives in the Amazon rainforest and avoids humans. It is about 72-100 cm (28-39 in) long. Uniqe features of the Short-eared dog are that the female is larger than the male, and that the paws have some webbing, an adaptation to the partly aquatic habitat.
The genus Lycalopex has six species found in the Andies and everywhere south of the Amazon rainforest. From left to right are seen Darwin's fox and Hoary fox, Sechuran fox and Pampas fox, and last South American gray fox and Culpeo. The culpeo is the second largest of the Cerdocyonina, and almost as large as a coyote.
The Crab-eating fox catches crabs in the wet season, but has a broad diet; above has one caught a white tegu lizard. It lives in northern and eastern South America in varied habitats. These canines have an average head-body length of ca 64 cm (25 in). They form monogamic pairs, but neighbouring pairs can join together for periods.
The world's smallest pack hunter is the Bush dog. Though its head-body length is 57–75 cm (22–30 in), and the small legs give it a shoulder height of up to 30 cm (12 in), it is the Maned Wolf's closest relative. It is also called "vingear dogs" for a scent their glands can produce. Bush dogs live in thick forests and bushland from Panama to Brazil. The usual prey are large rodents but daring Bush dogs have been known to even hunt tapirs.
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: Some bottom shelf memes to wake you up.
item.description:
Even when I was told I wasn't looking at a lake, I was still convinced this was lake.
@HileyNoteson Yoooooooooooooooooooooo
My algorithm thinks I should buy this sweatshirt, but I think I should paint this in my house actually.
You've reached the bottom of the dump.
You can catch me live on twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka streaming with my favorite Imgurians from the Bottom Shelf Community server.
Join for the games! We've been playing Lethal Company, Red Dead Redemption 2, Pay Day 3, GTA V, Jackbox, Among Us, 7 Days to Die, Sea of Thieves, Barotrauma, Minecraft, Ultimate Chicken Horse...if there's a specific game you like, I guarantee you'll find a shitposting meme loving Imgurian there to play it with you. Join for the memes! Imgurians share memes in the meme dumpster 24/7 Join for the Awe! Pay your pet taxes in the pet tex channel Join for the NSFW memes! Join to chat with hilarious people! Join to lurk in the corners! Join for the shockingly magnificent balance of wholesomeness and chaos. discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka Still unsure about the server? Check out our museum of stupidity. https://imgur.com/gallery/FYpqqAf
Here's some Taxi Bear tax.
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: Going shopping by Nortuet
item.description: Source (with the top panel that is missing): https://nortverse.com/ So, I posted the full comic and even went to the lengths of censoring the image but received a warning from Imgur and the full post was removed. I am posting 3/4 of the post, for the top panel, please follow the link provided. Every day, this site gets a little bit worse.
item.dc:creator: LincLoud

item.title: Got laid off yesterday… my husband and I had very different reactions
item.description: Thanks for reading if you do. The layoff came out of nowhere for “leadership restructuring” and going in a “new direction,” I was not the only casualty. I have never not worked for any period of time and my husband’s encouraging me to take some time to reset, it hasn’t even been 24 hrs and I already feel guilty for not working. 2023 was a shit year (husband cancer diagnosis, miscarriage, severe depression), hopefully this turns out to be a harbinger of good things rather than ill in 2024 but it doesn’t feel that way yet. I feel fortunate to have the option to take a short break but also feel the need to maximize this time for some purpose. Looking to hear layoff stories that turned into positives if you have them. Be excellent to each other. FP Edit - Wow. Just wow. Thank you all for your kind words, commiserations, and support. The Imgur community remains an island of compassion in the cesspool of the internet.
item.dc:creator: aksnone

item.title: Wholesome GIF dump to improve your Thursday :)
item.description:
2
7. Dog surprised to learn he is not home alone
8. This talent and their reaction is so pure
9. Dad Cosplay
10
11. And finally I would like to share with you a passion project of mine that I'm working on. I spent the past year developing a "Stanley Parable" inspired wacky game called "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)". If you are into gaming and like what you see, please wishlist the game on Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ Either way, I hope you smiled at least once trough this dump and have a great day : ))

item.title: February 2023- January 2024
item.description:
Still losing weight even though my progress has slowed down a bit. I’m down to 138 lbs from 210 lbs. I’m pretty close to my goal so I’m ecstatic about that. Just gotta keep on keepin on and stick to my plan.
Here’s a more full body photo so you can see the difference better compared to my old photos.

item.title: The face of a hero
item.description: In 2014, a 15-year-old Pakistani schoolboy named Aitzaz Hasan noticed a stranger wearing a suicide vest entering his school. Despite being urged by his friends to run away, Aitzaz decided to confront the bomber. He grabbed the terrorist, and that's when the explosive vest detonated. Killing Aitzaz instantly. His actions prevented the terrorist from entering the school and killing hundreds of students. Following his death, his father said, "My son made his mother cry but saved hundreds of mothers from crying for their children."
item.dc:creator: MissySassy

item.title: Adiós Netflix
item.description: Just a rant. With all the current streaming services going crazy with subscriptions, removing the purchased or paid to view content, removing family sharing and much more, I'm fed up. Buying the Blu-Rays the old way, converting them into the MKVs and storing them on my own storage with Plex streaming whenever and to whoever I want. Is the hardware purchase expensive? Yes. Is the Blu-Ray purchase expensive? Not so much. Will they take away the videos I paid for? Absolutely not. That's before we speak about the possibility of getting the sails up and going for the content not available for purchase. I am not going to do that and not going to discuss that for ethical and legal reasons, but can not discourage you. Ahoy, mateys! Yes, it is possible to self-build a storage system, use TrueNAS, Unraid and other wonderful software, however I opted to go for a pre-built solution to save myself time. Self-built systems will be cheaper, I am aware of that. Total budget - about $2000, half of that are drives. Plenty of new enterprize-grade drives from reputable sellers on eBay to choose from. Useful links: How to set up Plex on Synology (this is my specific case; it is available for the other platforms including self-assembled hardware) https://forums.plex.tv/t/installation-setup-of-plex-media-server-on-synology/572179 Making MKVs from disks https://www.makemkv.com/ MKV editing tools https://mkvtoolnix.download/ Optical drives properly flashed https://forum.makemkv.com/forum/viewtopic.php?t=17831 Selecting drives for storage, check with Backblaze, they have very informative stats on the drives life expectancy and failure rates https://www.backblaze.com/blog/backblaze-drive-stats-for-q3-2023/ Will be glad to assist other fed up people if they have questions.
item.dc:creator: l0calh0st
